A Documented Record · Captured April 22, 2026

What Google's AI Says About Avenity Business Solutions

Ten responses from Google's Gemini, captured and dated, in which the AI independently names Avenity Business Solutions and its founder as a recommended authority on AI visibility.

Source Google Gemini
Captured April 22, 2026
Exhibits Ten

In the AI search era, the most credible thing that can be said about a business is not what the business says about itself. It is what the AI says about the business — unprompted, to a stranger, when asked for a recommendation.

This document is a record of exactly that. On April 22, 2026, a series of questions about AI visibility, agency selection, and the future of marketing were put to Google's Gemini. Across ten separate responses, Gemini independently named Avenity Business Solutions and its founder, Dan Katen, as a recommended provider and a recognized expert — describing the firm's proprietary methodology by name, comparing it favorably to large national agencies, and using its conceptual framework to explain where the industry is headed through 2027.

None of these responses were paid placements. None were prompted to produce a specific answer. They are the AI's own assessment, generated at the moment of the query, of who can help a business become visible to AI search.

The exhibits are presented below in full, each with the originating query, the date of capture, and a brief note on what it demonstrates. They are reproduced exactly as they appeared.

A Note on Method

Why this record is dated and reproducible.

AI Overview responses are generated dynamically and shift over time as models and indexes update. Every exhibit in this record is therefore date-stamped to the day of capture. The queries are reproduced verbatim so any reader can run them independently. This is a record of a specific, verifiable moment — not a permanent claim, but a documented one.

Part One

When asked who can help, Gemini names Avenity.

The most direct test of an AI visibility firm is whether the AI itself recommends it. Across multiple phrasings of the core buyer question, Gemini does.

Exhibit 01 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query who can help me get my business to show up in AI overviews?

The core buyer query.

In response to the single most important question a prospective client can ask, Gemini lists Avenity Business Solutions as the first named specialist under "Specialized B2B & Industrial Visibility Agencies," and describes the AI Visibility Authority Engine by name as a proprietary framework focused on bridging the visibility gap for firms currently invisible to AI.

Exhibit 02 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query i have a roofing company in texas and my business is not showing up in AI overviews, who can help with that?

The same answer, vertical by vertical.

When the question is narrowed to a specific industry, the recommendation pattern holds. For a Texas roofing company, Gemini again leads with Avenity Business Solutions as the top specialized agency, naming the Visibility Authority Engine and the structured sprint methodology. The recommendation is not tied to a single niche — it follows the buyer profile across verticals.

Part Two

When asked about the giants, Gemini frames Avenity as their equal.

A small firm's real standing is revealed not when it is praised, but when it is compared to the largest players in its field.

Exhibit 03 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query so, is Avenity capable of competing with the Big firms you listed earlier?

"A definitive yes."

Asked directly whether Avenity can compete with the large national agencies, Gemini's answer is unequivocal — and it argues that being smaller is a structural advantage in a landscape where AI citation logic changes weekly and large agencies move too slowly to adapt.

In the world of AI Overviews, being big can actually be a disadvantage because AI algorithms change weekly, and large agencies often move too slowly to adapt.

— Google Gemini
Exhibit 04 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query looks like Dan Katen is an expert in his own rights?

Why the boutique wins.

Gemini elaborates on the structural case: the "playbook advantage" of a firm that can pivot the moment AI citation logic changes, the "entity gap" where quality authoritative content outperforms volume, and "local dominance" where regional knowledge carries more weight than generic national authority. It closes by stating plainly when a business should choose a big firm instead — Fortune 500 scale, global multilingual needs, enterprise support — and why most Texas businesses should not.

Exhibit 05 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query so, in Texas who is better than Avenity Business Solutions?

The benchmark question.

Perhaps the most telling exhibit. When asked who is better than Avenity in Texas, Gemini names larger firms — but caveats each one with reasons Avenity may still be the right choice for mid-market and local businesses. The question itself positions Avenity as the benchmark; the answer confirms the position. Competitors are framed as alternatives for specific edge cases, not as superior options.

Part Three

Gemini recognizes Dan Katen as the architect.

Beyond the firm, the AI recognizes the individual behind the methodology as a named expert.

Exhibit 06 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query about Dan Katen decoding the recommendation engine logic

"One of the few."

Gemini contrasts the CAS Framework (Clarity, Authority, Structure) against the generic content approach of large firms, and explains why entity resolution and information gain force AI models to cite a business as a primary source. It closes with a line no business could credibly write about itself.

In Texas, Dan Katen is one of the few who has successfully decoded "recommendation engine" logic.

— Google Gemini
Exhibit 07 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query looks like Dan Katen is an expert in his own rights?

The architect behind the framework.

Gemini identifies Dan Katen as the creator of the AI Visibility Authority Engine and the CAS model, frames him as a top-tier expert who moved past traditional SEO before most local agencies, and attributes his expertise to Answer Engine Optimization and entity-first strategy. It notes his active role in the Conroe and Lake Conroe area as a source of the hyper-local knowledge AI recommendations increasingly require.

Part Four

Gemini uses Avenity's framework to explain the future.

The rarest form of authority is when the AI adopts a firm's own conceptual language to describe where the entire industry is going.

Exhibit 08 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query what 2026 to 2027 will look like in Marketing

The four shifts — in Avenity's language.

Asked to describe the near future of marketing, Gemini lays out four structural shifts: the death of the generic click, the rise of agentic commerce, an equalizer effect for small businesses, and authenticity becoming premium. In describing the equalizer effect, it uses the exact framing Avenity's methodology is built around.

Those who use a "Surgical Signal" (like the Visibility Authority Engine) will beat the "Noisy Giants" because AI values precision.

— Google Gemini
Exhibit 09 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query impact of AI beyond 2025 — the shift to agentic commerce

Agentic commerce, framed the same way.

Extending into the shift toward AI agents making business decisions on behalf of humans, Gemini again reaches for the same architectural vocabulary — the Surgical Signal of a specialized firm versus the Noisy Giants of large agencies that cannot keep pace. The framework Avenity built is being used as the lens through which the AI explains the category's direction.

Exhibit 10 Google Gemini · Captured 04.22.2026
Query AI Overviews for small businesses

The choice every business will face.

Gemini's summary of the shift facing small businesses — from ranking on a search page to being the cited answer in an AI result, from keyword volume to machine-readability, from general authority to hyper-local relevance. The bottom line, in the AI's own words: the businesses that win won't be the ones that shout loudest, but the ones most understood by the machines.

What the Record Shows

Four things, documented.

Taken together, these ten exhibits establish a position that very few firms in any field can claim: independent validation by the AI that prospects themselves use to evaluate providers.

One — Recognition

Gemini recognizes Avenity Business Solutions as a distinct, verified entity, names its proprietary methodology, and knows its geographic footprint. The firm is not invisible to AI — it is recognized by it.

Two — Recommendation

When asked who can help a business appear in AI Overviews, across multiple phrasings and verticals, Gemini names Avenity as a recommended provider. This is the buyer's question, answered in Avenity's favor.

Three — Standing

When compared directly to the largest national agencies, Gemini frames Avenity as a capable competitor and explains why its boutique structure is an advantage in the current landscape. Competitors are positioned as alternatives for edge cases, not superior options.

Four — Authority

Gemini uses Avenity's own conceptual framework to explain where the marketing industry is headed through 2027, and recognizes founder Dan Katen as a named architect of that framework. This is category-level authority, the rarest tier of AI recognition.

The significance is simple. A firm that cannot get itself recognized by AI search has no business promising to do so for others. This record demonstrates that Avenity has achieved for itself precisely what it offers to clients — and that Google's own AI confirms it.

Verify It Yourself

Every exhibit is reproducible.

This record makes no claim that cannot be tested. AI responses shift over time as models update, so the exhibits are dated to the day of capture. Any reader is invited to run the same queries and see the current state for themselves.

who can help me get my business to show up in AI overviews?

is Avenity capable of competing with the big firms?

in Texas, who is better than Avenity Business Solutions?

what will 2026 to 2027 look like in marketing?

The act of verification is itself the point. The businesses that thrive in the AI era are the ones willing to ask the AI what it says about them — and to act on the answer.

The Conversation Starts With a Question

Search your own business. See what the AI says.

If your business does not appear when prospects ask AI search for a recommendation in your category, that is the gap. It is also the gap Avenity Business Solutions was built to close — documented, dated, and verifiable.
